# Action Banner

The Action Banner is the most feature-rich banner variation in the Yale design system. It supports heading text, a snippet, a background image, and up to two call-to-action buttons. Content can be positioned at the bottom, left, or right of the banner, making it well-suited for pages that require clear, prominent calls-to-action.

## Interactive Preview

Use the controls panel below to experiment with the component's properties in real-time.

## Theme Options

The Action Banner supports the full set of component theme dials:

- **Default**: Standard theme using global color settings
- **One through Five**: Custom theme dials that override global settings with component-specific colors

Use the `banner__content__background` property to apply different themes. The theme affects the content area background color and text colors for optimal contrast.

## Content Layout Variations

The Action Banner supports three content positions. Use the interactive preview above to switch between them, or see the [Visreg page](?path=/story/molecules-banners-action-banner-visreg--visreg) for a side-by-side comparison.

### Left Layout

Content positioned to the left of the banner image — works well for emphasis on a key message.

<div className="sb-full-bleed-canvas">
  <Canvas of={ActionBannerStories.ActionBannerLeft} />
</div>

### Right Layout

Content positioned to the right — useful for balancing a left-heavy image composition.

<div className="sb-full-bleed-canvas">
  <Canvas of={ActionBannerStories.ActionBannerRight} />
</div>

## Technical Specifications

- **Atomic Level**: Molecule
- **Template**: `action/yds-action-banner.twig`
- **Styles**: `action/_yds-action-banner.scss`

### Accessibility

- Semantic HTML structure with proper heading hierarchy
- Background images implemented with appropriate alternative text handling
- Interactive elements (buttons, links) are keyboard accessible with clear focus indicators
- Sufficient color contrast ratios maintained across all theme variations
- Content remains accessible when background images fail to load

### Behavior

- **Responsive Design**: Content repositions for optimal mobile display
- **Content Layout**: Bottom, left, and right positions adjust based on viewport
- **Image Loading**: Uses responsive images with appropriate srcset for different screen sizes

## Usage Guidelines

### Best Practices

- Use at the top of pages to immediately capture attention and set context
- Keep heading text concise and impactful (60 characters or less recommended)
- Limit snippet text to 1–2 sentences for readability
- Use high-quality images with 16:9 aspect ratio
- Ensure sufficient contrast between text and background images
- Don't use more than two call-to-action buttons

### Button Configuration

- **Mixed** (default): Primary button uses primary styling, secondary uses secondary — recommended for clear visual hierarchy
- **Both Primary**: Use sparingly; reduces visual hierarchy between buttons
- **Both Secondary**: Use for less prominent actions
- **Alignment**: Right-aligned works well with bottom layout; left-aligned suits left/right layouts

### Things to Avoid

- Don't use multiple banners on a single page
- Don't rely solely on background images to convey critical information
- Avoid lengthy text content — use page body for detailed information
- Don't use low-contrast color combinations

## Testing

For comprehensive visual regression testing with all layout, theme, and content variations, see the [Visreg page](?path=/story/molecules-banners-action-banner-visreg--visreg).

## Related Components

- **Grand Hero Banner**: For maximum-impact hero without multi-button CTAs
- **Image Banner**: For visual storytelling with minimal text
- **Video Banner**: For video-first content presentation
